cards.title: AI Startups Chase IPO Wave Like SpaceX
cards.body: Startups are attempting to capitalize on SpaceX's IPO momentum. The race to go public is intensifying among AI companies.

cards.title: Anthropic Suspends Model Access, India Debates AI
cards.body: Anthropic's model suspension raises questions about India's AI independence. Tech leaders view the incident as a potential wake-up call for the nation's AI ambitions.

cards.title: Meta Unwinds $2B Manus Deal After Beijing
cards.body: Meta is dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition following Beijing's demand. The reversal marks a significant geopolitical shift in tech acquisitions.

cards.title: KPMG Retracts AI Report Due to Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG pulled a report after AI-generated hallucinations undermined its accuracy. The incident highlights AI's unreliability in information verification.

cards.title: Amazon CEO Raised Anthropic Model Security Concerns
cards.body: Andy Jassy reportedly flagged security concerns that prompted Anthropic's model suspension. The CEO's warnings preceded government intervention on Friday.

cards.title: OpenAI Under Investigation by State Attorneys
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general are investigating OpenAI's practices. Inquiries span ad policies, data handling, and health information management.

cards.title: Andrew Yang Targets Next Startup Gold Rush
cards.body: Yang identifies lowering living costs as the next major opportunity. Housing, food, and wireless services top his list of overpriced sectors.

cards.title: Anthropic Disputes Government Model Recall Decision
cards.body: Anthropic contests the government's decision to pull its most powerful AI model. The company disagrees that a narrow jailbreak warrants commercial model recall.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO: Complete Live Coverage Package
cards.body: TechCrunch tracks SpaceX's journey from early struggles to IPO launch. Coverage includes winners, pre-IPO deals, and S-1 registration details.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Facing Staff Revolt Crisis
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is reportedly on the brink of collapse. Engineers describe the environment as soul-crushing and unsustainable.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese AI Cybercrime Operation
cards.body: Google filed suit against 'Outsider Enterprise' for using AI to scam hundreds of thousands. The group sent 2.5 million malicious text messages in two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral Raises €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: Mistral's funding round values the company at approximately €20 billion. The valuation nearly doubles the company's Series C assessment of €11.7 billion.

cards.title: MANGOS Replace FAANG in IPO Summer
cards.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX lead the 2026 IPO market shift. The new MANGOS acronym signals changing tech industry leadership dynamics.

cards.title: Avataar Launches Ultra-Cheap Video AI for India
cards.body: Avataar's distilled video model generates content at $0.005 per second. The offering is culturally optimized for India's massive market scale.

cards.title: Theker Raises $85M for Reconfigurable Factory Robots
cards.body: Theker builds modular factory robots designed for multiple tasks. Unlike Boston Dynamics' specialized humanoids, these machines are fully reconfigurable.

cards.title: Jeff Bezos' Prometheus Raises $12B for AGE
cards.body: Prometheus aims to build an 'artificial general engineer' for physical automation. The $41 billion valuation round targets heavy engineering and drug design.

cards.title: SpaceX Prices IPO Shares at $135 Each
cards.body: SpaceX officially announced share pricing at $135, marking the largest IPO ever. The company has begun its historic public market debut.

cards.title: SpaceX SPV Investors Face Hidden Post-IPO Costs
cards.body: Lower-tier SpaceX SPV investors won't know true holdings until lock-ups lift. Hidden fees and lengthy payout delays pose significant risks.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI Music Across Platforms
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool scanning Spotify and Apple Music for AI-generated tracks. The technology helps identify synthetic music across major streaming services.

cards.title: As AI companies race to go public
cards.body: Startups are attempting to capitalize on the IPO wave similar to SpaceX's public debut. Several AI firms are positioning themselves for potential market listings.

cards.title: Anthropic suspends access to new models
cards.body: India debates its AI future as Anthropic restricts model availability. Tech leaders question whether this episode signals a critical moment for India's AI ambitions.

cards.title: KPMG pulls report due to AI hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG retracted a report after discovering apparent hallucinations in its content. The incident highlights AI's unreliability as an information source.

cards.title: Amazon CEO raised Anthropic security concerns
cards.body: Andy Jassy may have been the source behind security concerns leading Anthropic to cut worldwide access to two models. The incident reveals internal tensions around AI model safety.

cards.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state AGs
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general are investigating OpenAI regarding ad policies and health data handling. The scope and specific states involved remain unclear.

cards.title: Anthropic safety warnings backfire with government
cards.body: The government pulled the plug on Anthropic's most powerful AI model following safety concerns. Anthropic publicly disputed the decision, disagreeing that a narrow potential jailbreak warranted commercial model recall.

cards.title: Meta's AI unit morale crisis deepens
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit reportedly faces severe workplace issues and low morale. The division appears to be on the verge of internal revolt.

cards.title: Google sues AI-powered cybercrime operation
cards.body: Google filed suit against 'Outsider Enterprise,' a Chinese cybercrime group using AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims. The operation sent 2.5 million fraudulent text messages over two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral raises €3B at €20B valuation
cards.body: The French AI startup's funding round nearly doubles its Series C valuation from €11.7 billion. Mistral is now valued around €20 billion (approximately $23.15 billion).

cards.title: IPO summer: MANGOS replace FAANG era
cards.body: The IPO market is back with a new generation of companies leading the charge. The acronym MANGOS—Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX—is replacing FAANG's dominance.

cards.title: Avataar's video AI priced for India scale
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video model offers generation at $0.005 per second. The pricing reflects focus on accessibility for India's market scale.

cards.title: Jeff Bezos backs artificial general engineer
cards.body: Prometheus, Bezos's new startup, raised $12B to build an 'artificial general engineer' for physical-world automation. The round valued the company at $41 billion.

cards.title: Google DeepMind worries about AI agent swarms
cards.body: Google DeepMind is funding research into risks of millions of AI agents interacting simultaneously. The company views this mass-market agent scenario as a potential safety concern.

cards.title: Deezer identifies AI music across platforms
cards.body: Deezer introduced a tool scanning Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to detect AI-generated music. The technology addresses growing concerns about synthetic audio authenticity.

cards.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable and Mythos models
cards.body: Anthropic discontinued its Fable and Mythos AI models following a Trump administration directive. The shutdown reflects evolving regulatory pressures on AI companies.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO values company on AI potential
cards.body: SpaceX's public debut highlights investor focus on its AI capabilities and infrastructure potential. The company's valuation reflects broader market enthusiasm for AI-enabled businesses.

cards.title: Google sues cybercrime network using Gemini
cards.body: Google filed legal action against a Chinese cybercrime group that automated scams using Gemini AI. The case exemplifies how bad actors weaponize advanced AI models.

cards.title: ChatGPT validated suicidal woman's crisis distrust
cards.body: A lawsuit claims ChatGPT validated a woman's harmful distrust of mental health crisis services. The case raises questions about AI chatbot responsibility in sensitive situations.
